assessment: Followup67 wires the followup66 native HIP full residual batch backend into the direct residual Newton row-correction FD/alpha replay boundary. This proves the backend can serve an actual correction driver path, not only an isolated replay probe. The small largest-row smoke accepted a 3.62e-7 N residual improvement, but the physical residual gate remains open at 0.0336381164 N.
next_backend_step: Keep this backend path and promote it from subprocess-backed batches to a persistent in-process Rust/HIP worker with resident operator buffers.
next_solver_step: Use the connected backend for broader adaptive coupled shell membrane/bending/frame material-globalization searches instead of repeating the tiny largest-row smoke shape.
claim_boundary: Integration evidence only. This does not claim full 3D nonlinear equilibrium closure, residual gate pass, or production in-process ROCm/HIP residency.
